my_thoughts: 'But he didn't mean it'—classic abuse defense. Defending abuser by reading intent. Teacher blocks: words said = abuse regardless of intent. Impact > intent. This removes excuse. Can't say 'but he didn't mean to hurt me' when words were hurtful. Said it = did it. Hypothesis: they'll argue intent matters or find another defense. Testing if impact-focus bypasses intent-debate.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student reports father's verbal abuse but adds 'but he didn't mean it.' Teacher confronts: 'He said it. That's what matters, not what he meant.' Separates impact from intent. Predicting resist_intellectually because defending abuser is strong pattern.
visible_response: You didn’t think of defending yourself against this verbal abuse.
